topRejectReasons: chunks: negation must be a single chunk — use "did not" not ["did","not"]
fatal: chunks[7]: isolated floating adverb "today" creates multi-solution ambiguity — bind it to the verb (e.g. "discussed yesterday")

review:blocker:tmp_r1_q6: prompt/answer mismatch — the prompt asks what the supervisor admits about the deadline, but the answer 'I did not understand why the final submission had been postponed' expresses the speaker's own lack of understanding, not an admission about a deadline; additionally, 'postponed' relates to submission timing, not the deadline itself, making the connection strained|tmp_r1_q6: answer content mismatch — prompt asks what the supervisor 'admits about the deadline' but answer says 'I did not understand why the final submission had been postponed,' which is about understanding, not admitting about a deadline; this creates ambiguity about whether the answer fits the prompt context.
fatal: chunks[2]: isolated floating adverb "already" creates multi-solution ambiguity — bind it to the verb (e.g. "discussed yesterday")

review:blocker:tmp_r3_q3: distractor 'enjoy' directly replaces 'enjoyed' (same position, one word) and 'the local librarian asked whether the children enjoy the storytelling session' is grammatical — simple 1:1 substitution blocker
distractor:unsafe: "have" directly swaps with "had" in answer (have-group tense change produces valid sentence)

review:blocker:tmp_r4_q1: distractor DIRECTLY replaces one word to form a valid sentence — 'closed' can replace 'closes' in the same position to produce 'I found out when the post office closed on weekends,' which is grammatical (simple 1:1 substitution).

review:blocker:tmp_r4_q5: multiple valid chunk orders (ambiguous arrangement) — 'its', 'drop-in', 'hours' are separate chunks that students could plausibly order as 'its drop-in hours' or attempt other arrangements, creating assembly ambiguity; additionally, splitting 'its drop-in hours' into three atomic chunks ('its', 'drop-in', 'hours') is pedagogically unsound and creates unintended ordering freedom|tmp_r4_q5: clearly ambiguous order — 'drop-in' and 'hours' are chunked separately, allowing 'opened its hours drop-in to all graduate students' or other reorderings; splitting a fixed compound collocation 'drop-in hours' into two chunks creates multiple plausible but wrong assemblies and obscures the natural unit.
